data.content:
**Diagnosis:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never adds it to the `@(Compile)` item group, so the C# compiler doesn't know about it.

**Fix:** Add a `<Compile Include>` for the generated file inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `@(Compile)` item group. MSBuild collects the list of files to compile before `CoreCompile`, and the generated file isn't in that list.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target using `<ItemGroup>` with a `<Compile Include="..." />` element.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  </Target>", "new_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <ItemGroup>

data.content:
**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never added it to the `@(Compile)` item group. MSBuild had already globbed `**/*.cs` before the target ran, so the late-generated file was invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup><Compile Include="..." /></ItemGroup>` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation. This works because MSBuild allows item group modifications inside targets that run `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"`.
